title: Cucumber Mint Tea Sandwich
description: Cool, herby cucumber sandwiches with plant cream or hummus. Classic tea-time plate, light and fresh.
explanation: Tea-time food does not have to mean biscuits and fried snacks. Thin cucumber sandwiches with mint and a creamy plant spread feel special enough for guests and light enough for afternoons.
Use soft sandwich bread or whole-wheat; crusts optional. Hummus, mashed avocado, or thick plant yogurt all work as the base.
ingredients: 3 tbsp Hummus or thick Plant Yogurt
Black Pepper and Salt to taste
Optional: thin tomato slices or chaat masala dust
instructions: Stir mint, lemon, salt, and pepper into hummus or plant yogurt.
Spread the mixture on bread slices.
Layer cucumber evenly. Sprinkle a pinch of chaat masala if using.
Close sandwiches, trim crusts if desired, and cut into triangles or fingers.
Serve immediately with tea, or wrap for a short carry.
stepNotes: Pat cucumber dry so bread stays soft, not wet.
Salt cucumbers lightly and wait 2 minutes, then pat dry for extra moisture control.
Assemble close to serving time for best texture.
Toasted bread holds up better if packing for later.
Whole-wheat or multigrain raises fiber; white bread feels more party-classic.

variationSections.title: Spread ideas
items: Peanut chutney for a spicier Indian tea-time.
Avocado mash with lemon and chilli flakes.
Tofu cream cheese style spread blended with herbs.

variationSections.title: Make it more filling
items: Add tofu slices or leftover chana mash.
Serve with roasted chana or fruit on the side.
